title: Published export points at a non-existent build artifact
reasoning: The package export declares ./dist/calibrate.js and ./dist/calibrate.d.ts, but the build config emits files from rootDir src/main into outDir dist. The included source file is src/main/calibrate.ts, so tsc will emit dist/calibrate.js only if the compiler root is src/main and the file is directly inside it; this part is consistent. However the package name is marked private and this feature is identified as a Node package. If it is intended to be packed or depended on as @codenuke/calibrate, private:true prevents publication and npm pack consumers cannot treat it as a public package. This is a release hazard for the feature as described, but may be intentional if all packages/* are strictly internal.
recommendation: Clarify whether @codenuke/calibrate is internal-only. If it should be published or installed independently, remove private:true and ensure the workspace release process includes it. If it is internal-only, keep private:true and do not classify it as a public package artifact.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The runtime and math tests import through the workspace path mapping and exercise source code, not npm publication behavior or package publishability.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a packaging smoke test that runs npm pack for packages/calibrate only if it is intended to be publishable, then imports the packed package from a temporary install.

history.note: packages/* are intentionally private internal implementation modules bundled into the public apps/cli tarball; @codenuke/calibrate is not an independently published package.
